main_keyword.volume_estimate: 1k-5k US/mo [ESTIMATE]; broader parent 'screen size calculator' likely 10k-30k US/mo [ESTIMATE]
main_keyword.volume_source: No keyword-tool data was capturable in SERPs. Derived from public signals observed via WebSearch: (1) at least 6 dedicated micro-tool pages rank for the exact phrase (screenresolutionchecker.com/monitor-size-calculator, calcpedia.com/monitor-size-calculator.html, sagecalculator.com/monitor-dimensions-calculator, screen-size.info, seethrudisplays.com, multimonitorcalculator.com) — that density of purpose-built pages implies commercially interesting volume; (2) Omni Calculator (claims 18M+ visits/mo across ~3,000 calculators, i.e. low-thousands avg per calculator page) maintains separate Screen Size, Screen Ratio, PPI and TV Size calculators, implying each variant clears their build threshold; (3) 'screen size calculator' SERP is broader and more contested (Omni, inchcalculator, toolstud.io, prodisplay), suggesting it is the higher-volume head term.

supporting_keywords.volume_estimate: 10k-30k US/mo [ESTIMATE] — most contested SERP observed (Omni, inchcalculator, toolstud.io, screen-size.info, prodisplay all have dedicated pages)
low thousands US/mo [ESTIMATE] — sagecalculator.com and calcpedia.com built exact-match pages for it
not directly observable; prinds.com/tools/screenDimensions and planetcalc rank for diagonal-to-width-height queries
likely 10k+ US/mo for 'ppi calculator' [ESTIMATE] — Omni, calculatorsoup, inchcalculator, sven.de/dpi all maintain dedicated pages
high [ESTIMATE] — Omni has a standalone page; frequently bundled with screen-size tools in SERPs

strong demand signal: 10+ dedicated ranking pages observed (monitorsizecomparison.com/monitors/27-inch, measuringstuff, calconi, innobytech, onecomputerguy, techbullish, splaitor, even AliExpress SEO wiki articles)
observable demand: calconi.com per-size cm pages, remarkt 'how many cm is 24 inch monitor', Quora thread all rank
dedicated pages observed on monitorsizecomparison.com, dimensions.com, Quora, plus 2 AliExpress SEO-wiki articles targeting it
PCPartPicker forum thread + monitorsizecomparison.com + easycompare.app + dimensions.com pages observed
an entire exact-match domain exists (monitorsizecomparison.com) plus displaywars.com, screensizechecker.com, howbigg.com — strong tool-intent demand
RTINGS has a dedicated editorial page (24-vs-27-inch); displaywars has both permutations as static URLs
RTINGS dedicated page (27-vs-32-inch) observed ranking for multiple queries in this cluster
usecalcpro, andreabrandi.com, hometheaterengineering, RTINGS size-to-distance all rank — adjacent tool demand

supporting_keywords.keyword: how wide is a 27 inch monitor
supporting_keywords.volume_estimate: question-form query returns full SERP incl. RTINGS, displaywars, HP Tech Takes
distinct intent: TV cluster is size-to-VIEWING-DISTANCE (RTINGS, Omni tv-size-distance, inchcalculator) not diagonal-to-dimensions; keep separate page if targeted

questions: How wide is a 27 inch monitor?
What are the dimensions of a 24-inch monitor?
How many cm is a 24 inch monitor?
Is a 32 inch monitor too big for gaming (or FPS/competitive play)?
Is monitor size measured diagonally, and does it include the bezel?
How far should I sit from a 27 inch monitor?
Why does a 34-inch ultrawide feel smaller than a 27-inch 16:9 monitor?
Is 1080p resolution good enough on a 32 inch monitor?
How much desk space (width and depth) does a 32 inch or 34 inch monitor need?
What monitor size should I get for my viewing distance?
What is the actual screen area difference between a 24 inch and 27 inch monitor?

landing_pages.rationale: Highest-density size query observed: 10+ dedicated ranking pages including AliExpress programmatic SEO articles chasing it — clear demand worth an exact-match page.
Calconi, remarkt, Quora, measuringstuff and monitorsizecomparison.com all rank for cm/inch variants; strong metric-conversion sub-intent (US + international spillover).
Dedicated pages on monitorsizecomparison.com, dimensions.com and two AliExpress wiki articles observed; pairs with the 'too big for gaming' question for FAQ pickup.
PCPartPicker forum thread ranking (weak content = easy win); 21:9 math is non-obvious, exactly what the calculator differentiates on.

landing_pages.target_keyword: monitor size comparison (24 vs 27 vs 32 vs 34)
landing_pages.rationale: Entire exact-match domain (monitorsizecomparison.com) plus displaywars/easycompare rank — validates the comparison-table feature as its own landing page.
Omni, calculatorsoup, inchcalculator and sven.de/dpi each keep standalone PPI pages; our tool already computes PPI so this page is near-free to add.
Calconi runs a whole per-size 'X-inch in cm' page series that ranks — signals sustained inch-to-cm conversion demand for screens.
